architecture_fit.note: Hand-built Fourier function solves the task perfectly but has no attention/transformer at all, so it never addresses the goal's 'does attention implement it' question or stay close to base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Only baseline is random 1/p from the benchmark metrics; no in-attempt ablation/strawman (e.g. no-exp or single-frequency) showing where the mechanism is necessary.

faithfulness.note: No model exists to ablate/patch and the README proposes no causal check; the Fourier mechanism is faithfully implemented but there is no evidence any learned model uses it.

operating_range.note: Sweeps moduli 11->113 (~1 order of magnitude) with constant 1.0 accuracy, but perfection is by construction so no degradation edge is revealed.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire attempt is a clean hand-set Fourier/circular circuit reproducing modular addition exactly — exactly the kind of by-hand mechanism this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is text-only (Textboxes for prediction and an embedding table); the bar chart of the logit vector promised in the README is absent.

visualisation_rationale.note: The 'Why this visualisation' section argues for a logit bar chart with a sharp peak and sinusoidal wrap, but that chart is not implemented — rationale describes a viz that doesn't exist.

notes: A correct, deterministic hand-built Fourier/circular circuit that hits 1.0 accuracy across the full modulus sweep and earns the hardcoded-weights bonus. But it is pure NumPy with no attention, transformer, or delta from base_model.py, so it sidesteps the goal's core question of whether attention implements modular addition, and offers no causal/faithfulness evidence. The visualisation is text-only and does not match the bar chart its README claims, weakening the demo further.
